function.name: bash
function.description:
Execute bash commands and wait for completion. Commands run non-interactively (stdin is closed).

Use this for:
- Directory exploration: ls, find, tree
- File inspection: cat, head, tail, wc, file
- Content search: grep, rg
- Development tools: bundle, rails, rake, ruby, gem, node, npm, yarn, git (requires approval)

IMPORTANT: For Rails generators and similar tools, always use --force or --skip flags to avoid interactive prompts:
- rails generate authentication --force (overwrites files)
- rails generate model User --force
- bundle install --quiet

Examples:
- grep -rn 'button' app/views
- find . -name '*.rb' -type f
- rails generate authentication --force
- git status

Auto-approved commands (no user confirmation needed): ls, pwd, find, tree, cat, head, tail, wc, file, which, echo, grep, rg

command.description:
The bash command to execute. Examples:
- 'grep -rn "button" app/views' (search for text in files)
- 'find . -name "*.rb"' (find files by name)
- 'ls -la app/' (list directory contents)
